verdict_reason: The engineering layer preserves the saṁyamī / muniḥ technical Sanskrit terms by name (verbatim retention in the body) and preserves the Shankara structural reading of the inversion (paramārtha-sat vs vyāvahārika). Although the engineering analog scopes 'substrate' to 'technical truth' and 'surface' to 'political weather' (an operational specification, not a tradition-flattening), it does not collapse the Advaita / Vishishtadvaita axis. The source pack preserves both readings explicitly: Shankara's paramārtha-vs-vyāvahārika and Ramanuja's Self-as-locus-of-devotion-vs-sense-objects. The engineering analog correctly stays at the operational layer where the doctrinal axis is irrelevant. The falsifiability clause ('most quiet engineers are simply unprepared') and the counter-example (personnel decisions where surface IS substrate) both demonstrate the kind of careful guarded reading an Indian-philosophy-aware writer produces. No conflation. Pass.
